rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFIORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION: $589.25 U.S. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner'S First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 6, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFIORNIA Motion To Compel Answers To Petitioner's First Set Of Demand For Production And Inspection Of Documents; Petitioner'S First Set Of Special Interrogatories; Judicial Council Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to MSchreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LEUNG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notrice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 6, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate Continued to October 23, 2014. Petitioner's motion was previously taken off calendar on August 12, 2014 for failure to provide a courtesy copy of the administrative record. Because the motion was taken off calendar, the Court no longer has courtesy copies of the papers filed in this matter. All parties shall deliver courtesy copies of the papers they filed to Dept. 302 by October 9, 2014 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. The Court is in receipt of the administrative record provided by Petitioner. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JESSICA RODRIGUEZ VS. CALIFORNIA UNEMPLOYMENT INSURANCE APPEALS BOARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 6, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF JESSICA RODRIGUEZ's Hearing On Verified Petition For Writ Of Mandamus Denied. The Administrative Record supports the Decision by the California Unemployment Insurance Appeals Board that Petitioner was discharged for misconduct and is therefore not eligible for unemployment benefits pursuant to Cal. Unemp. Ins. Code, sec. 1256. The evidence establishes that Petitioner was repeatedly tardy to work in violation of her employer's attendance policy, even after she received counseling, warnings, and suspensions due to the excessive violations. (Administrative Record, Ex. F, Transcript of Hearing, Exs. 2-7 to 2-14.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN MCGLYNN VS. ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 6, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT JOHN REAMPULLA Motion For Terminating Discovery Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: HEARING REQUIRED. THE PARTIES SHALL APPEAR TO ADDRESS WHETHER IT WOULD BE AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION FOR THE COURT TO GRANT TERMINATING SANCTIONS. IT APPEARS THAT THE FAILURE TO PROVIDE RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY AS ORDERED ON JUNE 26, 2014, WERE ALREADY ADDRESSED THROUGH THE ISSUE SANCTIONS ADOPTED BY THE COURT ON AUGUST 13, 3014. THE ONLY OTHER OUTSTANDING ISSUE OF NON-COMPLIANCE WITH DISCOVERY ORDERS IDENTIFIED BY PLAINTIFF, THE NON-PAYMENT OF MONETARY SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO THE JUNE 26, 2014, AND AUGUST 13, 2014, ORDERS, CANNOT SEEM TO SUPPORT TERMINATING SANCTIONS BASED ON THE HOLDING BY THE COURT OF APPEAL THAT IT WOULD BE AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION TO GRANT TERMINATING SANCTIONS BASED SOLELY ON A PARTY'S FAILURE TO PAY MONETARY SANCTIONS. NEWLAND V. SUPERIOR COURT (1995) 40 CAL.APP.4TH 608, 615 ("¿ A TERMINATING SANCTION ISSUED SOLELY BECAUSE OF A FAILURE TO PAY A MONETARY DISCOVERY SANCTION IS NEVER JUSTIFIED."). Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 6, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT EVAN BAILYN's Motion To Compel Arbitration Denied. rulings.Case Title: PACHARIN PISANSATHERAWONGE VS. KANE DANIEL MINKUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, October 6, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTS KANE MINKUS and KANE MINKUS' Notice Of Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint Motion denied. Defendant admitted in his declaration that he was personally served while present in California. (Minkus Decl. 3:6-9.) Nothing more is required for this Court to exercise jurisdiction over Defendant. (Pennoyer v. Neff (1877) 95 U.S. 714, 722.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
